repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: "[Process exited]" extmark is implemented in c with anonymous namespace and users have no way to hide it.

Solution: add new handler to `nvim.terminal` group to display the msg on `TermClose` event.

Ref: [#38315 (comment)](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/discussions/38315#discussioncomment-16145681)

comment_data.body: I'm not sure if forcing `TermClose` will have some unintended behavior, but its needed for this pr to move forward.
minor note, but could we just put the virtteext near the bottom of the window?

Can we avoid the `b.pending_exitmsg` state by defining a `++once` TermOpen handler here? 

```lua
  vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('TermOpen', {
    buffer = ev.buf,
    once = true,
    callback = function()
      set_terminal_exitmsg(ev.buf, msg, pos)
    end
  })
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -578,6 +578,49 @@ do
     end,
   })
 
+  local nvim_terminal_exitmsg_ns = vim.api.nvim_create_namespace('nvim.terminal.exitmsg')
+
+  --- @param buf integer
+  --- @param msg string
+  --- @param pos integer
+  local function set_terminal_exitmsg(buf, msg, pos)
+    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(buf, nvim_terminal_exitmsg_ns, pos, 0, {
+      virt_text = { { msg, nil } },
+      virt_text_pos = 'overlay',
+    })
+  end
+
+  vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('TermClose', {
+    group = nvim_terminal_augroup,
+    nested = true,
+    desc = 'Display the "[Process exited]" virtual text',
+    callback = function(ev)
+      if not vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(ev.buf) then
+        return
+      end
+
+      local buf = vim.bo[ev.buf]
+      local pos = ev.data.pos ---@type integer
+      local buf_has_exitmsg = #(
+          vim.api.nvim_buf_get_extmarks(0, nvim_terminal_exitmsg_ns, 0, -1, {})
+        ) > 0
+
+      -- `nvim_open_term` buffers do not have any attached chan
+      local msg = buf.channel == 0 and '[Terminal closed]'
+        or ('[Process exited %d]'):format(vim.v.event.status)
+
+      -- If BufFilePre/BufFilePost blocks and the process exits before
+      -- `terminal_open` could be called, then TermClose is queued before
+      -- TermOpen. Don't diplay the msg now, let TermOpen display it.
+      if buf.buftype ~= 'terminal' or buf_has_exitmsg then
+        vim.b[ev.buf].pending_exitmsg = { msg = msg, pos = pos }

comment_data.body:
afaict, if some autocommands block (without `nested=true`), the `TermClose` might be dropped due to `autocmd_busy`. 

```lua
local api = vim.api
api.nvim_create_autocmd('TermOpen', { command = "echomsg 'TermOpen'" })
api.nvim_create_autocmd('TermClose', { command = "echomsg 'TermClose'" })
api.nvim_create_autocmd('BufFilePre', { command = 'sleep 1' })
api.nvim_command('term ls')
```
 
In this scenario, `:term ls` only prints `TermOpen` and `TermClose` is "lost". So whether the exit msg is displayed or not now becomes dependent on whether the blocking autocommand is enabled with `nested=true` , unless we force it.

> why wasn't it needed before?

As for that, the only `default-autocmds` that handle `TermClose` automatically closes "A terminal buffer started with no arguments (which thus uses 'shell') and which exits with no error", as per docs. Shells with no args usually don't exit without user input so this wasn't an issue.

But even that handler can fail in an *extremely* hypothetical scenario like:
```
SHELL=/usr/bin/echo VIMRUNTIME=./runtime/ ./build/bin/nvim --clean    
``` 
and using the above lua repro, executing `:term` won't close the buffer automatically.

> Perhaps only force it when status >= 0

I think that can be done
I chose this as its the current behaviour and to avoid updating tests
This is fine for now. It's a bit weird to have an undocumented field. We can consider removing it in a followup PR.
